Here's a Python script demonstrating manual control of garbage collection cycles using the `gc` module:

```python
import gc
import sys

class Example:
    """Example class to demonstrate object lifecycle"""
    def __init__(self, name):
        self.name = name
        print(f"{self.name}: Created")
    
    def __del__(self):
        print(f"{self.name}: Destroyed")

def demo_gc_control():
    """Demonstrate manual garbage collection control"""
    
    print("=== Garbage Collection Control Demo ===\n")
    
    # 1. Check current GC settings
    print("1. Initial GC Status:")
    print(f"   GC enabled: {gc.isenabled()}")
    print(f"   GC thresholds: {gc.get_threshold()}")
    print(f"   GC count: {gc.get_count()}")
    
    # 2. Create cyclic references (garbage)
    print("\n2. Creating cyclic references...")
    a = Example("Object A")
    b = Example("Object B")
    a.ref = b  # Create reference
    b.ref = a  # Create cyclic reference
    
    # Delete references (objects become unreachable)
    del a
    del b
    
    # 3. Disable automatic GC
    print("\n3. Disabling automatic garbage collection...")
    gc.disable()
    print(f"   GC enabled: {gc.isenabled()}")
    
    # 4. Manual collection with different methods
    print("\n4. Manual collection methods:")
